files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.CidKeyedFont
files.Summary: Very small loader for CID-Keyed Type 1 PostScript fonts. Only a subset of the format is implemented. Refer to Adobe Technical Note 5014 for the full specification.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.PostScriptFont
files.Summary:
Very small PostScript font loader based on a custom text representation. The intention is to mimic a subset of the Type 1 syntax without parsing the full PostScript language. Each glyph is declared as shown below:

Glyph: A
Width: 500
Height: 600
Baseline: 0
Path:
M 0 0
L 10 0
...
EndGlyph

For a description of the real Type 1 format see Adobe Technical Note 5040.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.PostScriptGlyph.PathCommand
files.Summary: Represents a single drawing command extracted from the font. The coordinates use the same units as the original charstring.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.PostScriptGlyph.PathCommandType
files.Summary: Enumeration of possible path command types supported by this simplified glyph representation.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.PostScriptGlyph
files.Summary: Represents a glyph defined by a sequence of basic PostScript path commands. The commands correspond loosely to the operators described in Adobe's Type 1 specification.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.Type3Font
files.Summary: Minimal loader for PostScript Type 3 fonts. Only a tiny subset of the language is interpreted: setcachedevice for metrics and the basic path commands moveto, lineto, curveto and closepath. See Adobe Technical Note 5015 for the full details.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.PostScript.Type42Font
files.Summary: Minimal loader for PostScript Type 42 fonts which encapsulate a TrueType font. This implementation extracts the embedded TrueType data from the /sfnts array and delegates glyph retrieval to . For the official format see Adobe Technical Note 5012.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.HeadTable
files.Summary: The 'head' table contains global information about the font. It records facts such as the font version number, creation and modification dates, revision number, and basic typographic data that applies to the font as a whole. This includes the font bounding box, the primary writing direction, and other information regarding glyph placement within the em square. The checksum is used to verify the integrity of the font data and can help distinguish between similar fonts.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Hinting.TtfHintingContext

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Acnt.AcntFormat0
files.Summary: Represents the ACNT format 0, which provides specific accent attachment information for a TrueType font glyph.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Acnt.AcntFormatBase
files.Summary: Serves as the base class for ACNT (Accent) formats in a TrueType font table.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable.AccentDescription.Multiple
files.Summary: Format 1 description: two or more secondary components referenced via the extension sub-table. Stored as 6 bytes: UInt16 primary word (bit15=1), Int32 absolute extension offset.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable.AccentDescription.Single
files.Summary: Format 0 description: a single secondary (accent) component identified by a direct table index. Stored as 4 bytes: UInt16 primary word, byte attachment point, byte secondary index.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable.AccentDescription
files.Summary: Represents the description of an accented glyph, associating a primary glyph with one or more accent attachments.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable.ExtensionEntry
files.Summary: A single entry in the extension sub-table (2 bytes), referencing one secondary glyph attachment. Bit layout: [bit15=isLast | bits14-8=SecondaryInfoIndex(7) | bits7-0=PrimaryAttachmentPoint].

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable.SecondaryEntry
files.Summary: A single entry in the secondary (accent) data table (3 bytes): Int16 secondary glyph index followed by one byte attachment point.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AcntTable
files.Summary: The accent attachment table (tag: 'acnt') provides a space-efficient method of combining component glyphs into compound glyphs to form accents. Accented glyphs are a very restricted subclass of compound glyphs.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AvarTable.AxisValueMap
files.Summary: A single input→output coordinate mapping entry within a . Both values are F2Dot14 (divide by 16 384 to obtain the real normalized value).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AvarTable.SegmentMap
files.Summary: The piecewise-linear segment map for one design axis. Must include mappings for –1→–1, 0→0 and +1→+1 as required by the spec.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.AvarTable
files.Summary: The 'avar' (Axis Variations) table maps normalized design-axis coordinates through per-axis piecewise-linear segment maps, allowing non-linear axis progressions and ensuring that certain canonical normalized coordinates (−1, 0, +1) map to themselves.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.BslnTable.BslnFormat

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.BslnTable

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.CMapFormat0

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.CMapFormat4.DeltaMap
files.Summary: Represents a segment defined by a delta value (no explicit mapping table).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.CMapFormat4.Segment
files.Summary: Represents a segment in the CMap Format 4 mapping table.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.CMapFormat4.TableMap
files.Summary: Represents a segment with an explicit mapping table.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.CMapFormat4
files.Summary: Represents a CMap Format 4 for mapping characters to glyph indices using segmented arrays.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CMap.CMapFormatBase
files.Summary: Represents the base class for CMap (character to glyph mapping) formats in a TrueType font.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CmapTable.CmapSubtable
files.Summary: Represents a subtable identifier defined by a platform ID and platform-specific ID. Used as a key for the cmap subtables.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CmapTable
files.Summary: Represents the 'cmap' table which provides the character-to-glyph mapping for a TrueType font. The table contains one or more subtables (each identified by a platform ID and platform-specific ID) that define different mapping formats.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CvarTable.TupleVariation
files.Summary: One tuple variation record: covers one region of the variation space and carries the CVT deltas to apply when a font instance falls inside that region.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CvarTable
files.Summary: The CVT variations table (tag: 'cvar') provides delta adjustments to CVT values for different instances of a variable font. It uses the tuple variation store format: each tuple variation covers one region of the variation space and carries packed deltas for the CVT entries it affects.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CvtTable
files.Summary: The CVT (Control Value Table) contains a series of signed 16-bit integers that are used as control values during the hinting process. These values are used by the font program to adjust glyph outlines.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.DsigTable
files.Summary: The 'DSIG' (Digital Signature) table contains one or more cryptographic signatures that attest to the authenticity and integrity of a font. Most tools treat this table as an opaque blob: they preserve it on read and discard or regenerate it on write. This implementation stores the entire table as raw bytes after the 12-byte outer header.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FdscTable.FontDescriptor
files.Summary: One named descriptor entry: a 4-byte tag and a 16.16 fixed-point scalar value.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FdscTable
files.Summary: The 'fdsc' (Font Descriptors) table provides a set of named scalar values that describe characteristics of the font — such as optical weight, contrast, or size. Each descriptor has a 4-byte tag and a 16.16 fixed-point value.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FeatTable.FeatureNameRecord
files.Summary: Describes one AAT layout feature, its flags, its name-table entry, and its available settings.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FeatTable.FeatureSetting
files.Summary: One named setting for a feature (e.g. "On", "Off", "Standard Ligatures").

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FeatTable
files.Summary: The 'feat' (Feature Name) table lists the Apple Advanced Typography (AAT) layout features supported by the font and their available settings. Each feature has a numeric identifier, a name-table entry, and one or more named setting values.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FmtxTable
files.Summary: The 'fmtx' (Font Metrics) table provides glyph-based typographic metrics: it identifies a reference glyph whose phantom points carry the horizontal and vertical metric data, and stores the indices of those phantom points within the glyph.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FpgmTable
files.Summary: The FPGM (Font Program) table contains a set of instructions (bytecode) that are executed once when the font is loaded. These instructions establish routines used for hinting glyphs.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FvarTable.InstanceRecord
files.Summary: Represents one named instance (a specific point in the design space).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FvarTable.VariationAxisRecord
files.Summary: Describes one design axis of the variable font.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.FvarTable
files.Summary: The 'fvar' (Font Variations) table defines the design axes and named instances for a variable font. Each axis specifies a minimum, default and maximum design-space coordinate, and each named instance represents a specific position in the design space (e.g. "Regular", "Bold", "Light").

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GaspTable.GaspBehavior
files.Summary: Rasterization behaviour flags for a gasp range. Version 0 supports only and ; version 1 additionally supports and .

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GaspTable.GaspRange
files.Summary: Describes the recommended rendering behaviour for one PPEM size range.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GaspTable
files.Summary: The 'gasp' table contains information which describes the preferred rasterization techniques for the typeface when it is rendered on grayscale-capable devices at various sizes. Each entry specifies a maximum pixels-per-em threshold and the corresponding rendering behaviour.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Glyf.GlyphBase
files.Summary: Represents the base class for a TrueType glyph.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Glyf.GlyphCompound
files.Summary: Represents a compound glyph in a TrueType font. A compound glyph is composed of multiple simple glyph components, each with its own transformation.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Glyf.GlyphSimple
files.Summary: Represents a simple (non‑compound) glyph in a TrueType font.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GlyfTable
files.Summary: The 'glyf' table contains the data that defines the appearance of the glyphs in the font. It includes the specification of the points that describe the contours that make up each glyph outline as well as the instructions for grid-fitting the glyph. The table supports both simple glyphs and compound glyphs (i.e. glyphs composed of other glyphs).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HdmxTable.DeviceRecord
files.Summary: Contains per-glyph advance widths (in pixels) for one specific PPEM size.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HdmxTable
files.Summary: The 'hdmx' (Horizontal Device Metrics) table stores pre-computed advance widths for specific pixels-per-em sizes, allowing rasterizers to avoid recomputing them at render time. Each covers one PPEM size and contains one advance-width byte per glyph.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HheaTable
files.Summary: The 'hhea' table contains information needed to layout fonts whose characters are written horizontally, that is, either left-to-right or right-to-left. This table provides global metrics for the font, including typographic values such as ascent, descent, line gap, and other essential data used during glyph layout.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.HmtxTable
files.Summary: The 'hmtx' table contains metric information for the horizontal layout of each glyph in the font. It begins with the hMetrics array, where each entry consists of the advance width and left side bearing for a glyph. The number of long horizontal metrics is specified in the 'hhea' table, and in a monospaced font, only one entry is required (but must still be present).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.KernTable
files.Summary: Represents the 'kern' table which contains kerning pairs for adjusting the spacing between glyphs in a TrueType font. This implementation supports the most common format (version 0) with a single horizontal kerning subtable.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LcarTable
files.Summary: The 'lcar' (Ligature Caret) table specifies caret positions within ligature glyphs so that a text caret can be placed at intermediate positions inside a ligature (e.g. between the two characters of a fi ligature). Each ligature glyph has one or more caret values.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LocaTable
files.Summary: The 'loca' table stores the offsets to the locations of the glyphs in the font relative to the beginning of the 'glyf' table. Its purpose is to provide quick access to the data for a particular glyph. For example, in the standard Macintosh glyph ordering, the character A is the 76th glyph in a font. The 'loca' table stores the offset from the start of the 'glyf' table to the position at which the data for each glyph can be found.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LtshTable
files.Summary: The 'LTSH' (Linear Threshold) table stores a per-glyph pixel-per-em threshold value. Below this threshold the glyph is non-linear (i.e. hinting is used); at or above it the glyph scales linearly and hinting can be suppressed for better appearance. A value of 1 means the glyph is always linear; 0 means the threshold is unknown.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.MaxpTable
files.Summary: The 'maxp' table establishes the memory requirements for a font. It begins with a table version number and the number of glyphs in the font. The remaining entries set maximum values for various parameters (such as maximum points, contours, and storage), which are used to allocate resources for the font.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.OpbdTable.OpticalBounds
files.Summary: Optical bound adjustments for one glyph (left, top, right, bottom), either in design units (format 0) or contour-point indices (format 1).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.OpbdTable
files.Summary: The 'opbd' (Optical Bounds) table provides per-glyph adjustments that shift the effective optical edge of a glyph inward or outward, improving the visual alignment of text at the margins. Each glyph can have four adjustments: left, top, right, and bottom.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Os2Table.EmbeddingFlags
files.Summary: Embedding permission flags stored in .

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Os2Table.SelectionFlags
files.Summary: Font style selection flags stored in .

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.Os2Table
files.Summary: The 'OS/2' table contains metrics and other data required for correct rendering on Windows and OS/2 platforms. It is present in virtually every TrueType/OpenType font and describes properties such as weight, width, style, typographic metrics, Unicode character ranges and vendor identification.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.PcltTable
files.Summary: The 'PCLT' table contains PCL 5 characterization data: information needed to identify and render the font on PCL 5-capable printers. The table has a fixed size of 54 bytes.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.PostTable
files.Summary: The 'post' table contains information needed to use a TrueType font on a PostScript printer. It provides data for the FontInfo dictionary, the PostScript names for all glyphs, and memory usage information for the PostScript driver.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.PrepTable
files.Summary: The PREP (Control Value Program) table contains instructions (bytecode) that are executed before rendering any glyph. These instructions typically perform scaling and other pre-processing needed for proper hinting.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.PropTable
files.Summary: The 'prop' (Glyph Properties) table assigns directional and typographic property bits to each glyph. Properties include writing direction (LTR/RTL/neutral), hanging punctuation, kerning suppression, and attachment class.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.TrakTable.TrackData
files.Summary: One direction's worth of tracking data (horizontal or vertical).

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.TrakTable.TrackEntry
files.Summary: One entry in the tracking table: a nominal tracking value and a name-table entry, with per-size spacing adjustments.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.TrakTable
files.Summary: The 'trak' (Tracking) table specifies inter-character spacing adjustments as a function of point size. For each nominal tracking level (e.g. "loose", "normal", "tight") a set of per-size values (in font design units) gives the amount to add to each glyph's advance width.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.VheaTable
files.Summary: The 'vhea' table contains information needed to lay out fonts whose characters are written vertically, that is, either top-to-bottom or bottom-to-top. It is the vertical analogue of the 'hhea' (horizontal header) table and has the same 36-byte fixed structure.

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.VmtxTable
files.Summary: The 'vmtx' table contains metric information for the vertical layout of each glyph in the font. It is the vertical analogue of the 'hmtx' table. Its layout consists of a series of longVerMetric records (advance height + top side bearing) followed by additional top-side-bearing-only values for the remaining glyphs. The number of long-metric entries is given by , and the total glyph count by .

files.Title: Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tag
files.Summary: Represents a 4-byte identifier used in font file formats such as TrueType (TTF). Encodes a string of up to 4 ASCII characters into a 32-bit integer. Provides implicit conversions and equality comparisons with strings, integers, and byte arrays.

files.Summary: Represents a vector of displacement on a spherical geodesic with a bearing (heading direction).

files.Summary: Represents an immutable map position defined by geographical coordinates and a zoom level.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Model.Planet<T>
files.Summary: Represents a planet with geodesic properties, such as equatorial radius and circumference.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Model.Planets<T>
files.Summary: Contains predefined planet models with their respective equatorial radii.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Model.ProjectedPoint<T>
files.Summary: Represents an immutable point in a projected coordinate system with an associated projection transformation.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.EquirectangularProjection<T>
files.Summary:
A simple Equirectangular (Plate Carrée) projection that uses degrees throughout:

X = longitude (in degrees) Y = latitude (in degrees)

Hence (0°, 0°) => (0,0) with no offsets or scalings. If latitude exceeds ±90, it is optionally clamped.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.GallsPetersProjection<T>
files.Summary:
Implements the Gall–Peters cylindrical equal-area projection "centered" at (lat=0°, lon=0°) => (x=0, y=0).

Formulas (in degrees): x = cos(45°) * longitude y = sin(latitude) / cos(45°)

lon = x / cos(45°) lat = asin( y * cos(45°) )

Both input (GeoPoint) and output (ProjectedPoint) use degrees.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.IProjectionTransformation<T>
files.Summary: Represents a generic transformation interface that converts objects to objects and vice versa.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.LambertAzimuthalEqualArea<T>
files.Summary: Implements the polar aspect of the Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection for spherical coordinates: the projection is centered on the north pole (latitude=90°), which maps to (x=0, y=0), not on the equator/prime meridian.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.MercatorProjection<T>
files.Summary:
centered Mercator projection where (lat=0°, lon=0°) maps to (X=0, Y=0),

Forward transform: X = lon Y = ln( tan(45° + lat/2) )

Inverse transform: lon = X lat = 2 * [ atan( exp(Y) ) - 45° ]

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.MillerProjection<T>
files.Summary: Implements the Miller cylindrical map projection.

files.Title: Utils.Geography.Projections.MollweideProjection<T>
files.Summary:
Mollweide (Homalographic) equal-area projection, implemented with degree-based trigonometry.

Lat/Lon in degrees => (x,y) in "Mollweide units" with:

  • (0°,0°) maps to (0,0).
  • The bounding ellipse is roughly x ∈ [-2√2..+2√2], y ∈ [-√2..+√2].

Usage: var projector = new MollweideProjectionDegrees<double>(); var projected = projector.GeoPointToMapPoint(new GeoPoint<double>(latDeg, lonDeg)); var unprojected = projector.MapPointToGeoPoint(projected);

files.Title: Utils.IO.PartialStream
files.Summary:
Provides a view onto a restricted portion (a "slice") of an underlying . This partial stream starts at a specified offset within the base stream, and has a maximum length. Reading or writing beyond that partial length is not permitted.

The property reflects the offset within the partial stream, not the offset in the underlying stream. All read and write operations are bounded by the specified length. The underlying stream's position is restored after each operation, so external consumers of the base stream see no position change (though locking is used to ensure thread safety in concurrent scenarios).

By default, disposing this does not close or dispose the underlying stream.

files.Title: Utils.IO.StreamCopier
files.Summary:
A writable-only stream that copies written data to multiple target streams simultaneously.

This class implements both and so that the set of target streams can be dynamically modified at runtime. Note that no concurrency control is provided; if multiple threads write concurrently, external locking may be needed.

Reading and seeking are not supported. Calling will broadcast the provided data to all underlying streams in the targets collection.

By default, disposing this object does not dispose any of the contained streams. If the parameter is true when constructing this class, all target streams will be disposed when is called.

files.Title: Utils.IO.StreamUtils

files.Title: Utils.IO.StreamValidator
files.Summary:
A write-only buffering stream that defers writing to a target stream until is called. Data written to this stream is held in an internal buffer. When is invoked, the buffered data is flushed into the underlying target stream. If is called, the buffered data is discarded without being written to the target.

This class does not support reading or seeking. After you finish all writes, you can either commit them by calling or discard them via . The underlying stream is not closed or disposed automatically when this stream is disposed (override if you need that behavior).

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Line<T>

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.MathEx

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.NullableIntEx
files.Summary: Provides helper methods for working with nullable numeric endpoints that can represent open ranges by treating null as positive or negative infinity as needed.

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.NumericalMethods

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.Polynomial<T>
files.Summary: Represents a polynomial with coefficients of type T. Coefficients are stored as [a₀, a₁, …, aₙ] representing a₀ + a₁x + … + aₙxⁿ.

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.Statistics
files.Summary: Descriptive statistics computed over sequences of floating-point values.

files.Title: Utils.Mathematics.Trigonometry<T>
files.Summary: Provides a base implementation of for custom angle measures such as Degrees or Grades, along with default trigonometric/hyperbolic implementations.

files.Title: Utils.Net.Arp.ArpOperation

files.Title: Utils.Net.ArpUtils
files.Summary: Provides helpers to build ARP packets.

files.Summary: Provides access to the server contexts during command execution.

files.Title: Utils.Net.CommandResponseClient
files.Summary: Provides a base client for text based command/response protocols.

files.Title: Utils.Net.CommandResponseServer
files.Summary: Provides a base server for text based command/response protocols.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.Algorithm
files.Summary: Enumerates algorithm identifiers used in DNS security (DNSSEC) and related cryptographic functions. Defined primarily for keys and signatures in DNSSEC records.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.CertificateTypes
files.Summary: Enumerates certificate types that might appear within CERT DNS records.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DHCIDIdentifierTypes
files.Summary: Enumerates identifier types for DHCID records (DHCP Identifier), distinguishing how a client or device is identified in DHCP.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSCanonicalWriter
files.Summary: Provides functionality to serialize DNS packets into a array using reflection and expression trees. This class can handle standard DNS headers, request records, response records, and user-defined DNS response details annotated with .

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSClassId
files.Summary: DNS class identifiers that describe the scope of a resource record.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSConstants
files.Summary: Constants representing DNS datagram flags and fields masks.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSDatagram
files.Summary: Represents a DNS datagram, which provides methods for reading and writing DNS-related data. It supports simple DNS name compression, reading and writing integers of various sizes, and handling string labels in DNS-compatible formats.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSDomainName
files.Summary: Represents a DNS domain name composed of an optional subdomain and a parent domain. Provides comparison and equality checks against both and .

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSElement
files.Summary: Represents the abstract base class for all DNS elements within this library.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSElementsComparer
files.Summary: Provides an implementation for objects.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSError
files.Summary: Four bits that indicates the returned error value (4 bits, aligned)

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSFactory
files.Summary: Provides a mechanism to discover and store DNS-related types, along with their associated class and record IDs. This class uses metadata to map DNS record classes and IDs to corresponding .NET types that implement .

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSFieldAttribute
files.Summary: Applied to properties or fields within a DNS record class to indicate how the corresponding data should be serialized, deserialized, or otherwise interpreted.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader
files.Summary: Represents the DNS header section of a DNS packet, including standard bits from RFC 1035 §4.1.1 and the AD/CD extensions introduced in RFC 2535. The header provides information about flags, counts for queries/answers/authorities/additionals, and other DNS-level metadata.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeadersComparer
files.Summary: Provides an implementation for objects.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSOpCode
files.Summary: Requested operation (4bits, aligned)

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSPacketReader
files.Summary: Provides helpers to translate DNS datagrams into strongly typed response structures.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSPacketWriter
files.Summary: Provides functionality to serialize DNS packets into a array using reflection and expression trees. This class can handle standard DNS headers, request records, response records, and user-defined DNS response details annotated with .

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSQRBit
files.Summary: Identifies whether the DNS datagram carries a query or a response payload (1 bit, aligned).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSRecordAttribute
files.Summary: Specifies a DNS record attribute that can be applied at the class level to identify the DNS , record ID, and an optional descriptive name.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSRequestRecord
files.Summary: Represents a DNS query (request) record within a DNS packet, following the layout defined in RFC 1035 §4.1.2 and indicating which record type (e.g., A, AAAA, MX) is being queried for a particular domain name.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSRequestType
files.Summary: Request type identifiers used in legacy AXFR-style zone transfers.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSResponseDetail
files.Summary: Represents the base class for DNS response details (RData). Concrete types that inherit from this class are annotated with for identifying DNS record types and include fields marked by for serialization.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSResponseRecord
files.Summary: Represents a DNS response record, containing all necessary fields to describe a specific DNS answer in a response packet, including the domain name, the record type, TTL, and the , as defined by RFC 1035 §4.1.3.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSText
files.Summary: Provides helper methods to convert DNS records to and from the textual representation commonly used in zone files.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DNSTextRecordAttribute
files.Summary: Specifies the textual format for a DNS record. The format string may contain property or field names enclosed in curly braces (e.g. {Preference}) that will be replaced when converting to or from text.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.DigestTypes
files.Summary: Enumerates digest/hash algorithms for DNSSEC-related records, such as DS (Delegation Signer).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.FieldsSizeOptions
files.Summary: Enumerates the supported prefixed-size mechanisms for array or field lengths.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.GatewayType
files.Summary: Enumerates gateway types used in IPSECKEY records to indicate the nature of the gateway (e.g., an IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or a domain name).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IANAddressFamily
files.Summary: Enumerates IANA address family types used in DNS-based IPSECKEY or other resource records that store numeric address family identifiers.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IDNSReader<T>
files.Summary: Describes a component capable of reading DNS headers from a transport-specific source.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IDNSWriter<T>
files.Summary: Represents a DNS serializer targeting a specific transport type.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.IPSecAlgorithm
files.Summary: Enumerates cryptographic algorithms for IPSECKEY records.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.Protocol
files.Summary: Enumerates the protocols associated with a DNSKEY (or KEY) resource record, specifying how the key is intended to be used (e.g., for TLS, email, DNSSEC).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.Address
files.Summary: Represents an IP address record (A, AAAA, or NSAP) in DNS, as described by RFC 1035 §3.4.1 (A records), RFC 1886 (superseded but historically defining AAAA records), and the OSI NSAP address type. This class stores the IP address and reflects the correct numeric record ID based on the address family.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.CNAME
files.Summary: Represents a DNS CNAME (Canonical Name) record, used to alias one domain name to another, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.1.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.Default
files.Summary: Represents a fallback or "default" DNS record for scenarios where the record type is unrecognized, or when a custom processing path is desired for type code zero (which is reserved/unused). Despite the TXT-like comment, this class is annotated with [DNSRecord(DNSClass.IN, 0x00)], indicating it is treated as a placeholder for type 0. The raw payload is stored in the property.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.HINFO
files.Summary: Represents an HINFO (Host Information) record, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.2.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MB
files.Summary: Represents an MB (Mailbox) DNS record, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.3 (marked as experimental).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MD
files.Summary: Represents an MD (Mail Destination) record, which is an obsolete DNS record type as per RFC 1035 §3.3.4.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MF
files.Summary: Represents an MF (Mail Forwarder) record, an older mail-routing DNS record type now considered obsolete per RFC 1035 §3.3.5. Similar to MD, the MF record has been superseded by MX.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MG
files.Summary: Represents an MG (Mail Group) record, classified as experimental in RFC 1035 §3.3.6.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MINFO
files.Summary: Represents a MINFO record, considered experimental and largely obsolete as per RFC 1035 §3.3.7. The MINFO record provides mail-related information (responsible mailbox, error mailbox) for a mailing list or mailbox owner.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MR
files.Summary: Represents an MR (Mail Rename) record, marked as experimental and obsolete as per RFC 1035 §3.3.8.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MX
files.Summary: Represents an MX (Mail Exchange) record in the DNS, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.9 and further clarified by RFC 974. The MX record specifies a mail server responsible for accepting email on behalf of a domain, along with a preference value.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.NS
files.Summary: Represents an NS (Name Server) record as specified in RFC 1035 §3.3.11. An NS record indicates the authoritative name server for a given domain.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.NULL
files.Summary: Represents a NULL (experimental) DNS record as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.10. This record type can hold arbitrary data (up to 65535 bytes) and is primarily a placeholder for experimental or interim purposes.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.PTR
files.Summary: Represents a PTR (Pointer) record in DNS, as specified by RFC 1035 §3.3.12.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.SOA
files.Summary: Represents an SOA (Start of Authority) record, which defines zone-wide parameters including the primary name server, mailbox of the zone administrator, and various time intervals affecting caching and zone transfers, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.13.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.TXT
files.Summary: Represents a TXT record in DNS, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.14. TXT records store arbitrary human-readable text, often used for descriptive or application-specific data (e.g., SPF, DKIM, etc. in modern usage).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.WKS
files.Summary: Represents a WKS (Well Known Services) record, as specified by RFC 1035 §3.4.2. A WKS record details which well-known services are available for a given IPv4 address and IP protocol (e.g., TCP or UDP).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.AFSDB
files.Summary: Represents an AFSDB (Andrew File System Database) record, as defined in RFC 1183 §1.2. This record helps locate AFS or related database servers for a particular domain.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.ISDN
files.Summary: Represents an ISDN (Integrated Services Digital Network) DNS record, as originally defined in RFC 1183 §3.2. This record stores an ISDN telephone number (and optionally a subaddress).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.RP
files.Summary: Represents an RP (Responsible Person) record, as defined in RFC 1183 §2.2. This record identifies the mailbox of the person responsible for a particular DNS zone and an optional TXT record with additional information.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.RT
files.Summary: Represents an RT (Route Through) record, as defined in RFC 1183 §2.3. The RT record specifies an intermediate host that should be used when routing to the owner of the record, along with a preference value.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1183.X25
files.Summary: Represents an X.25 PSDN address record, as defined in RFC 1183 §3.1 (type code 19). This record stores a single X.25 PSDN address (e.g., a numeric string like "311061700956").

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1348.NSAP_PTR
files.Summary: Represents an NSAP-PTR record, which provides a mapping from a domain name to an NSAP address.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1712.GPOS
files.Summary: Represents a GPOS (Geographical Position) record as defined in RFC 1712. GPOS records store geographical location information for a domain, including longitude, latitude, and altitude.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1876.LOC
files.Summary: Represents a LOC (Location) record in DNS as defined in RFC 1876. The LOC record specifies the physical location associated with a domain name, including its geographical coordinates, altitude, and precision values.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2052.SRV
files.Summary: Represents an SRV (Service) record, which specifies the location of servers for a particular service and protocol within a domain. SRV records are defined in RFC 2782 (originally RFC 2052).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2230.KX
files.Summary: Represents a KX (Key Exchange) record in DNS as defined by RFC 2230. A KX record specifies a host that is willing to act as a mail exchange for the owner name, along with a preference value to indicate priority when multiple KX records are present.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.KEY
files.Summary: Represents a DNS KEY record (type = 25) as specified in RFC 2535 §3. The KEY record stores a public key and associated metadata (flags, protocol, algorithm).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.KeyOwner
files.Summary: Enumerates possible owner types stored in bits 6-7 of .

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.NXT
files.Summary: Represents an NXT (Next) record in DNS as specified by RFC 2535 §5.1.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2535.SIG
files.Summary: Represents a SIG (Signature) record in DNS as defined in RFC 2535. The SIG record is the fundamental mechanism by which data is authenticated in the secure DNS. It digitally signs an RRset (as defined in RFC 2181) and binds the data to a specific time interval and the signer's domain name.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2671.OPT
files.Summary: Represents an OPT (Option) record as defined in RFC 2671. OPT records are used to convey additional options and information in DNS messages, most notably as part of the EDNS (Extension mechanisms for DNS) protocol.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2672.DNAME
files.Summary: Represents a DNAME (Delegation Name) record as defined in RFC 2672. A DNAME record provides redirection for an entire subtree of the DNS namespace.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC2915.NAPTR
files.Summary: Represents a NAPTR (Naming Authority Pointer) record in DNS, as defined in RFC 2915. NAPTR records are used for flexible rewriting of domain names and are commonly employed in applications such as ENUM, VoIP, and Internet telephony to map telephone numbers to URIs.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC3123.APL
files.Summary: Represents an APL (Address Prefix List) record as defined in RFC 3123. An APL record is used to specify a list of address prefixes along with a negation flag, indicating which address ranges are included or excluded.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4025.IPSECKEY
files.Summary: Represents an IPSECKEY (IPsec Key) record, as defined in RFC 4025. This record can store IPsec-related data such as a precedence value, gateway type (IPv4, IPv6, domain name, or none), an IPsec algorithm, and a public key.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.DNSKEY
files.Summary: Represents a DNSKEY record as specified in RFC 4034. A DNSKEY record stores a public key used to verify DNSSEC signatures and is a critical component in the establishment of a chain of trust in DNSSEC.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.DS
files.Summary: Represents a DS (Delegation Signer) record as specified in RFC 4034. DS records are used to securely delegate authority from a parent zone to a child zone by providing a cryptographic digest of a DNSKEY record from the child zone.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.NSEC
files.Summary: Represents an NSEC (Next Secure) record as defined in RFC 4034. NSEC records are used in DNSSEC to provide authenticated denial of existence for a range of domain names and RR types. They create a chain of all the names in a zone, indicating which types exist for a given owner name and which names do not exist.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4034.RRSIG
files.Summary: Represents an RRSIG (Resource Record Signature) record as defined in RFC 4034. The RRSIG record is used in DNSSEC to authenticate RRsets by providing a digital signature that covers the RRset data.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4255.SSHFP
files.Summary: Represents an SSHFP (SSH Fingerprint) record as defined in RFC 4255. An SSHFP record stores a fingerprint of an SSH public key, allowing clients to verify the authenticity of an SSH server by comparing the fingerprint from DNS with the key provided during the SSH connection.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4398.CERT
files.Summary: Represents a CERT record, as specified in RFC 4398, "Storing Certificates in the DNS." CERT records are used to store certificates or Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) in DNS.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC4701.DHCID
files.Summary: Represents a DHCID (DHCP Identifier) record as defined in RFC 4701. The DHCID record is used by DHCP servers and clients to associate a DHCP client's identity with a DNS name, enabling deterministic dynamic DNS updates for a zone.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC5155.NSEC3
files.Summary: Represents an NSEC3 (Next Secure 3) record as defined in RFC 5155. NSEC3 records are used in DNSSEC to provide authenticated denial of existence while mitigating zone enumeration. They replace the earlier NSEC records by hashing the owner names.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC5155.NSEC3PARAM
files.Summary: Represents the NSEC3PARAM resource record describing NSEC3 hashing parameters for a DNS zone, as defined in RFC 5155 §4.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC5205.HIP
files.Summary: Represents a HIP (Host Identity Protocol) record as defined in RFC 5205. HIP records provide a way to associate a host identity with a public key and, optionally, a list of rendezvous servers. This mechanism is part of the Host Identity Protocol, which separates the roles of IP addresses as locators from host identities.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC6844.CAA
files.Summary: Represents a CAA (Certificate Authority Authorization) record, as defined in RFC 6844. A CAA record specifies which Certificate Authorities (CAs) are permitted to issue certificates for the owner’s domain.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.RFC7553.URI
files.Summary: Represents a URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) record as defined in RFC 7553. A URI record allows a domain to publish one or more URIs, potentially with priority/weight for load balancing or backup links.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.SSHAlgorithm
files.Summary: Enumerates algorithm types for SSHFP records (SSH Fingerprint records in DNS).

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNS.SSHFingerprintType
files.Summary: Enumerates fingerprint/hash algorithms for SSHFP records.

files.Title: Utils.Net.DNSLookup
files.Summary: A class for performing DNS lookups using specified name servers.

files.Summary: Client for the TCP Echo protocol (RFC 862).

files.Summary: Defines a serializer for instances.

files.Summary: Generic interface implemented by converters supporting a specific target type.

files.Title: Utils.Net.IMimePartConverter
files.Summary: Defines a converter capable of converting a MIME part body to a target type.

files.Summary: Provides access to newsgroup articles for the NNTP server.

files.Summary: Defines operations required by a POP3 server to access mailbox data.

files.Summary: Defines the interface used by to exchange messages.

files.Summary: Represents an error that occurs while sending or receiving ICMP packets.

files.Summary: Represents an ICMP packet, including creation, serialization, and parsing.

files.Title: Utils.Net.Icmp.IcmpPacketType
files.Summary: Defines well-known ICMP packet types used by the utilities.

files.Title: Utils.Net.Icmp.TracerouteHop
files.Summary: Represents a single hop returned by a traceroute operation.

files.Summary: Provides methods for sending and receiving ICMP packets, including Echo Requests and Traceroute.

files.Title: Utils.Net.IpRange
files.Summary: Represents a contiguous range of IP addresses. Instances are immutable and may be constructed from a pair of addresses or from a single address with a prefix length. The range can be enumerated to list each address it contains.

files.Title: Utils.Net.MailUtils
files.Summary: Provides helper methods for parsing addresses and populating recipients.

files.Summary: Represents a MIME document composed of multiple parts.

files.Summary: Represents a single MIME part.

files.Summary: Provides conversion of contents based on registered converters.

files.Summary: Factory used to convert objects to instances.

files.Summary: Provides methods for reading instances from various sources.

files.Title: Utils.Net.MimeType
files.Summary: Represents a MIME type with optional parameters.

files.Title: Utils.Net.MimeWriter
files.Summary: Provides methods for writing instances to various targets.

files.Title: Utils.Net.NetworkParameters
files.Summary: Provides access to host networking metadata such as interfaces and DNS servers.

files.Summary: Client for the Network News Transfer Protocol (NNTP).

files.Title: Utils.Net.NntpServer
files.Summary: Server for the Network News Transfer Protocol (NNTP).

files.Title: Utils.Net.NtpClient
files.Summary: Client for the Network Time Protocol (RFC 5905).

files.Summary: Client for the Post Office Protocol version 3 (POP3).

files.Title: Utils.Net.Pop3Server
files.Summary: Server for the Post Office Protocol version 3 (POP3).

files.Title: Utils.Net.QueryString.QueryValues
files.Summary: A helper class for working with multiple query values under a single key. It can be used like a list or implicitly converted to a comma-separated string.

files.Title: Utils.Net.QueryString
files.Summary: A collection class representing the key-value pairs in a query string, allowing multiple values per key and intuitive syntax for manipulation.

files.Title: Utils.Net.QuoteOfTheDayClient
files.Summary: Client for the Quote of the Day protocol (RFC 865).

files.Title: Utils.Net.ResponseSeverity
files.Summary: Indicates the severity of a server response.

files.Title: Utils.Net.ServerResponse
files.Summary: Represents a single server response line consisting of a code, its severity and an optional message.

files.Title: Utils.Net.SmtpAuthenticationMechanism
files.Summary: Specifies the authentication mechanism used with the and .

files.Title: Utils.Net.SmtpAuthenticationResult
files.Summary: Represents the result of an SMTP authentication attempt.

files.Title: Utils.Net.SmtpMessage

files.Summary: Simple server implementation for the Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP).

files.Summary: Represents a double precision value that can be encoded to and decoded from string or byte representations.

files.Summary: Client for the Time protocol (RFC 868).

files.Summary: Provides a convenient way to construct and manipulate URIs, including user credentials, query parameters, and fragments.

files.Title: Utils.Net.WakeOnLan
files.Summary: Provides helpers to send Wake-on-LAN magic packets.

files.Summary: Defines the unit and subunit names used to convert a currency amount to words.

files.Summary: Holds date-format configuration for a language.

files.Summary: Formatting options for the decimal part produced by .

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.DefaultNumberToStringLanguageSpecifics
files.Summary: Default no-op implementation used when no language-specific finalization is configured.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.DigitListType
files.Summary: Represents a collection of digit definitions for a particular group of numbers.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.DigitType
files.Summary: Describes how a specific digit should be rendered and composed within a number name.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.FormVariantType
files.Summary:
A variant node inside a form-producing element (<Replacement>, <OrdinalException>, <Ordinal>). Plays two roles depending on whether is present:

  • Intermediate node + add one constraint; child <Variant> elements cascade further constraints.
  • Leaf node + provide one output form per dimension value in the order declared by the matching <Dimension> element. Empty entries are skipped.

All (constraints, form) pairs are collected at load time and merged by constraint set into OrdinalVariantRule or VariantRule entries — no runtime changes required.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.FractionListType
files.Summary: Represents a collection of fractional definitions.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.FractionType
files.Summary: Configures how fractional values should be rendered.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.GermanNumberToStringLanguageSpecifics
files.Summary: Applies German writing-specific finalization rules to converted numeric texts.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.GroupType
files.Summary: Represents a numbered group of digits used when decomposing large numbers.

files.Title: Utils.NumberToString.GroupsListType
files.Summary: Collection wrapper for entries.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Bootstrap.Antlr4Grammar
files.Summary:
Builds the meta-grammar: a hand-coded capable of tokenizing and parsing ANTLR4 .g4 files.

This is the only in the project that is constructed programmatically rather than loaded from a .g4 file. It uses exactly the same model objects as any other grammar, demonstrating that the framework is self-describing.

The resulting definition must be passed through RuleResolver.Resolve before use.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Bootstrap.Antlr4GrammarConverter
files.Summary:
Converts a parse tree produced by into a fully-resolved .

The typical entry point is the static method, which runs the complete pipeline: tokenize → parse → convert → resolve.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.LexerCommandType

files.Summary: A named lexer mode containing an ordered list of lexer rules. The default mode is always named DEFAULT_MODE. Additional modes are declared via mode NAME; in the grammar. Rules are ordered by to ensure correct maximal-munch priority during tokenization.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.LiteralMatch

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.ModeSwitch

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.Negation

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.ParserDefinition
files.Summary:
Complete, immutable description of a grammar produced either by loading a .g4 file through Antlr4GrammarConverter or by constructing the meta-grammar programmatically (as done in Antlr4Grammar.Build()).

A is consumed by:

  • — to tokenize an input stream,
  • — to build a parse tree.

After construction, call RuleResolver.Resolve(definition) to populate and validate rule references.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.PrecedencePredicate
files.Summary: A precedence predicate of the form { precpred(_ctx, n) }?, used by ANTLR4 to handle left-recursive rules. The numeric level is parsed from the raw code rather than stored verbatim.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.Quantifier
files.Summary: A quantified repetition: , +, ?, or {n,m}. is true by default; setting it to false enables non-greedy matching (the ??, ?, +? suffixes).

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.RangeMatch
files.Summary: Matches any single character within an inclusive range, e.g. 'a'..'z'.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.Rule
files.Summary: A single grammar rule — either a lexer rule (upper-case name by convention) or a parser rule (lower-case name by convention).

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.RuleCatchClause
files.Summary: A parser rule catch clause preserved as passive metadata only.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.RuleContent
files.Summary: Abstract root for all grammar elements that can appear inside a rule body. Concrete subtypes fall into three categories: tokenizer content (character-level matching), composite structures (sequences, alternations, quantifiers), and meta-instructions (predicates, embedded actions, lexer commands).

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.RuleExceptionMetadata
files.Summary: Parser rule exception metadata preserved without runtime exception semantics.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.RuleKind
files.Summary: Identifies whether a rule belongs to the lexer or parser.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Model.RuleLabel

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Runtime.ParseTreeCompiler<TContext, TResult>
files.Summary:
Generic depth-first parse-tree compiler that separates traversal into two ordered phases for each node:

  1. Descent (top-down) Called when the node is first reached. Handlers can enrich the context for the subtree below — for example pushing a new scope, recording parameter names, or resolving a function signature before its body is compiled.
  2. Ascent (bottom-up) Called after all children have been compiled. Handlers receive the ordered list of child results and must return the compiled result for this node — for example folding two sub-expressions into a binary operation.

Handlers can be registered by grammar-rule name (exact match, O(1) lookup) or by an arbitrary predicate over the (checked in registration order). Rule-name handlers are always checked first; predicate handlers are tried only when no name-based handler matches. Fall-back Default* handlers are consulted last.

The context is treated as immutable per descent step: each descent handler receives the context that was active when the node was entered and must return the context to hand to the node's children. Returning the same instance is always valid; creating a derived instance (e.g. a new scope) isolates sibling subtrees.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Runtime.ParseTreeNavigator
files.Summary:
Provides fluent, index- and name-based navigation over a tree produced by .

Each navigation method returns a new wrapping the target node so that calls can be chained: 

var token = nav[0].Child("additionExp")[1][0].Token;

Methods that cannot guarantee a result have a Try variant that returns null instead of throwing.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Runtime.ParserEngine
files.Summary:
Builds a parse tree from a flat token list using the rules in a . This engine is the runtime authority for token consumption, diagnostics, parse-tree construction, and final parse outcomes.

The engine is a recursive-descent parser with full backtracking. It tries each alternative in priority order and rolls back the token position plus configured parser execution state on parser attempt-boundary failure. Left-recursive rules are handled by a cycle-detection stack that returns null when the same rule is re-entered at the same token position.

Additional runtime guards stop repeated parser-state exploration and non-progressive iterations in quantifiers and left-recursive extensions. Backtracking remains active in this implementation and may be revisited in a future PR.

Semantic predicates (, ) and embedded actions () are controlled by the configured . The default policy preserves conservative behavior: semantic predicates return and parser actions return . Custom injected policies may reject alternatives and may execute action handlers, which can influence parse outcomes.

Scheduling and metadata components do not own parse decisions: continuation descriptors are descriptive only, shared-prefix plans are informational only, and neither metadata pipeline executes runtime continuations.

files.Title: Utils.Parser.Runtime.StackParserRuleInvocationFrameManager
files.Summary: Stack-aware parser rule invocation-frame manager that tracks the active call chain.
